name: repo-intelligence
description: AI-powered repository analysis engine that deeply understands your codebase before generating context-aware code using local Ollama models.

repo-intelligence.provider.markdownDescription: Active model backend, by provider id. Use **Repo Intelligence: Choose Model Provider** to configure it — this is deliberately not an enum so new providers work without a schema change.

enumDescriptions: Most capable; best for long multi-file agent runs.
Near-Opus quality on coding at lower cost.
Fastest and cheapest; best for simple, scoped edits.
repo-intelligence.anthropic.model.description: Claude model used for agent runs.

repo-intelligence.anthropic.effort.markdownDescription: Reasoning depth and token spend. `xhigh` suits the hardest agentic coding; `low` suits quick scoped edits. Ignored by Claude Haiku 4.5, which does not support this setting.

repo-intelligence.ollama.contextWindow.markdownDescription: Context window requested from the local Ollama model. Sent as `num_ctx` and used to decide when to compact the agent transcript. Raising this lets the agent hold more of the repository in view, but the KV cache grows with it — set past what your GPU can hold and the model spills to CPU, which is far slower than a smaller window.

repo-intelligence.ollama.keepAlive.markdownDescription: How long Ollama keeps the model loaded after a turn, as a duration such as `30m`, or `-1` to keep it loaded indefinitely. Longer values avoid re-loading the model between questions at the cost of holding memory.

repo-intelligence.ollama.chatModel.markdownDescription: Local model used for the agent. Needs to be capable enough to emit structured tool calls — a small general-purpose model will ask you where files are instead of searching. Recommended: `qwen2.5-coder:7b` or larger. Pull one with `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b`.

repo-intelligence.search.enableEmbedding.markdownDescription: Generate embeddings during a scan to enable semantic search. Requires Ollama (the Anthropic API has no embeddings endpoint) and can take several minutes on a large repository. When off, retrieval uses keyword search only.

repo-intelligence.scan.excludePatterns.description: Glob patterns to exclude from scanning

repo-intelligence.scan.maxFileSize.description: Maximum file size in bytes to scan (default 512KB)

repo-intelligence.context.maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens to include in context for AI prompts

repo-intelligence.scan.autoScanOnOpen.description: Automatically scan the repository when the workspace opens

repo-intelligence.agent.maxTurns.description: Maximum model turns in a single agent run before it stops and reports back.

repo-intelligence.agent.maxOutputTokens.description: Maximum tokens the model may generate per turn. Capped to the model's own limit.

repo-intelligence.agent.commandTimeoutMs.description: Maximum execution time for an approved agent command

repo-intelligence.agent.maxChangeSetSize.description: Maximum file operations in one agent change set

repo-intelligence.agent.initialContextMa.description: Maximum indexed code sent before the agent uses follow-up tools

repo-intelligence.agent.ignorePatterns.description: Path segments that the coding agent must not access

repo-intelligence.agent.allowSessionTrus.description: Whether the UI may offer in-memory session trust; commands always require approval

repo-intelligence.providers.markdownDescription: Per-provider settings, keyed by provider id. Managed by **Choose Model Provider**. Never contains API keys — those live in the OS keychain via SecretStorage.

enumDescriptions: Use another configured provider when the selected one is unavailable.
Fail the run instead of silently switching backend.
repo-intelligence.providerFallback.description: What to do when the selected provider cannot be reached.

repo-intelligence.providerFallbackOrder.description: Provider ids to try, in order, when the selected one is unavailable. Empty means use each provider's own rank.

repo-intelligence.ui.reactPanel.markdownDescription: Use the rebuilt sidebar with an inline model picker, mode toggle and approval cards. Turn off to fall back to the previous UI if something goes wrong.

scripts.typecheck: tsc --noEmit && tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.test.json && cd webview-ui && npm run typecheck
scripts.test: vitest run && cd webview-ui && npm test
